summary: PR 2425 primarily decompiled and improved gmstaffroll code, adding large bodies for fn_801AAB74 and fn_801AB200, recovering staff-roll text/sort data structures, converting several raw accesses to HSD_Text/HSD_JObj fields, and renaming the baselib GX link-head global from HSD_GObj_804D7824 to HSD_GObjGXLinkHead across symbols, headers, baselib, and a few stage/library users. The only feedback present is an automated report showing progress on gmstaffroll functions and .sdata2; no human review comments or PR body were loaded.

key_files.role: Primary decomp target; adds implementations for fn_801AAB74 and fn_801AB200, typed staff-roll structures, includes, assert macro cleanup, HSD_Text/HSD_JObj field use, sorting and cursor/staff interaction logic.
Symbol metadata updates for gmstaffroll staff info buffers and HSD_GObjGXLinkHead.
Header declaration renamed from HSD_GObj_804D7824 to HSD_GObjGXLinkHead.
Baselib GObj implementation updated to use HSD_GObjGXLinkHead in declarations and GX-link iteration.
GX-link list manipulation updated to use HSD_GObjGXLinkHead.
GObj initialization allocation/zeroing updated for HSD_GObjGXLinkHead.
Stage user updated from HSD_GObj_804D7824 to HSD_GObjGXLinkHead.
Stage user updated from HSD_GObj_804D7824 to HSD_GObjGXLinkHead.
Library user updated from HSD_GObj_804D7824 to HSD_GObjGXLinkHead.

what_changed: Replaced gmstaffroll placeholders for fn_801AAB74 and fn_801AB200 with large C implementations.
Added staff-roll data types including staffInfo_t, staffInfoSortBuf_t, SortBufEntry, and StaffEntryData, plus static/external data declarations used by gmstaffroll.
Converted several generated padded text-window shapes to HSD_Text pointers and replaced x54 accesses with HSD_Text entity accesses.
Converted staffInfoSortBuf entries from a padded pointer shape to explicit index, jobj, mtx, and x38 fields.
Replaced raw __assert blocks in gmstaffroll with HSD_ASSERT calls at the same line-number evidence points.
Introduced and used the HSD_GObjGXLinkHead name for the GX link-head array in config symbols, baselib declarations/uses, and call sites in gmstaffroll, grpushon, grzebes, and lbshadow.
Added a Gm_GObj_GXLink_PlyCursor constant used in an HSD_ASSERT expression for GX link index 9.

Automated report recorded improvements for main/melee/gm/gmstaffroll: fn_801AB200 from 0.00% to 93.13%, fn_801AAB74 from 0.00% to 91.86%, .sdata2 from 4.08% to 94.00%, and gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter from 96.48% to 99.35%.
smart_moves: Recovered concrete library types where the diff had enough shape evidence: HSD_Text replaced local padded text-window structs, and HSD_JObj replaced a padded matrix-owner pointer inside the sort buffer.
Updated the owning baselib header and implementation users when renaming HSD_GObj_804D7824 to HSD_GObjGXLinkHead, rather than hiding the rename behind local declarations.
Mapped expanded assert blocks in gmstaffroll back to HSD_ASSERT macro form, preserving line numbers and assertion expressions.
Kept staff-roll sorting explicit with SortBufEntry records storing index, jobj, and transformed matrix, which made the depth-order bubble sort readable in the new decompilation.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When generated code exposes a padded text-window struct with an object pointer at a known offset, check whether an existing UI/text type already owns the fields; in this PR, HSD_Text allowed win entries and gm_80480D58/gm_804D680C to use entity instead of x54.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gmstaffroll HSD_Text win[2] and entity accesses

decomp_lessons.lesson: If a global data symbol is renamed for meaning, update the symbol table, owning header, implementation, and known users together so the project has one visible canonical name.
Assert string/line evidence can be used to collapse raw __assert blocks into HSD_ASSERT when the line number and predicate are preserved.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_GObjGXLinkHead
naming_conventions.meaning_supported_by: Renamed from HSD_GObj_804D7824 and used as an indexed head array for GX-linked GObjs in baselib and callers.

naming_conventions.name: staffInfo
naming_conventions.meaning_supported_by: Used in gmstaffroll as per-entry staff display state with win[0], win[1], and x8 fields.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gmstaffroll staffInfo declaration and uses

naming_conventions.name: staffInfoSortBuf
naming_conventions.meaning_supported_by: Used as the buffer of SortBufEntry records sorted by transformed matrix depth.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gmstaffroll SortBufEntry and bubble sort

naming_conventions.name: SortBufEntry
naming_conventions.meaning_supported_by: Contains index, jobj, mtx, and x38; instances are filled from visible staff JObjs and sorted.

naming_conventions.name: StaffEntryData
naming_conventions.meaning_supported_by: Contains check callback, check_arg, alt_page, has_check, and language-related bytes used for staff entry display decisions.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gmstaffroll StaffEntryData and fn_801AAB74/fn_801AB200 uses

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK retained in new gmstaffroll functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: fn_801AAB74 uses PAD_STACK(0x20) and fn_801AB200 uses PAD_STACK(0x60). The automated report shows score improvements, but no narrow objdiff/build log is loaded to prove each padding choice individually.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use HSD_ASSERT macro rather than raw __assert expansion.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The diff replaces expanded __assert calls with HSD_ASSERT at known gmstaffroll line numbers.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Recover typed fields before raw pointer math where possible, but leave some pointer-chain work in fn_801AB200.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The diff improves HSD_Text and SortBufEntry typing, while fn_801AB200 still contains void*/u8* pointer chasing for particle data and offset-based gm_803DBFD8 accesses in the truncated excerpt.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Depth sorting implemented as explicit nested loops over staffInfoSortBuf.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: fn_801AAB74 computes view-space matrices and bubble-sorts SortBufEntry records by mtx[2][3].

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Reported 4 improvements in unmatched items for GALE01: fn_801AB200, fn_801AAB74, .sdata2, and gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter.
No human review comments or review records were loaded for this PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching gmstaffroll fn_801AAB74 fn_801AB200 for cleanup after the 91-93% automated report.
Search HSD_GObjGXLinkHead to confirm whether the rename remained canonical and whether all HSD_GObj_804D7824 references were removed later.
Search StaffEntryData gm_803DBFD8 z symbol to clarify data symbol ownership and naming consistency.
Search staffInfoSortBuf SortBufEntry PAD_STACK for later removal or verification of stack padding.
Search particle pointer chains in fn_801AB200 for later type recovery of HSD_psAppSRT or particle internals.

notes: The full raw diff and raw PR JSON are marked truncated in loaded_files, but the changed-file list and targeted grep covered all nine changed files for high-level classification.
The PR body is empty and no human review comments are loaded, so reviewer intent and rationale cannot be inferred.
Automated report gives score improvements, not full build, objdiff, or regression evidence for each individual tactic.

The symbols diff renames gm_803DBFD8 to z while gmstaffroll source declares static StaffEntryData gm_803DBFD8[198]; this slice does not provide enough evidence to interpret z as an accepted semantic name.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use existing UI/text types to replace padded text-window structs
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2425 shows gmstaffroll cleanup where padded structs with x54 object pointers were replaced by HSD_Text* and accesses to ->entity. This is a candidate example for typed-fields-over-pointer-math when a known library type matches the layout and call pattern.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: HSD_Text win[2], gm_80480D58, gm_804D680C, entity accesses

accepted_candidate_records.title: Canonical global rename requires symbol/header/implementation/user alignment
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2425 renamed HSD_GObj_804D7824 to HSD_GObjGXLinkHead in symbols.txt, gobj.h, baselib implementation files, and stage/library users. This is a candidate example of avoiding alias-style global renames by updating references directly.

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/gm/gmstaffroll.c owns staff-roll UI/text decomp work
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2425's largest change is in src/melee/gm/gmstaffroll.c, adding implementations for fn_801AAB74 and fn_801AB200 and recovering staff-roll text display/sort structures.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential source-owned path fact for future searches; should be curated before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: HSD_GObjGXLinkHead is declared in baselib gobj surfaces
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2425 updates the GObj GX link-head global name across config symbols, src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h, gobj.c, gobjgxlink.c, and gobjinit.c, with users in gmstaffroll, grpushon, grzebes, and lbshadow.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path/symbol fact; current intake should not decide global canonical status beyond this PR evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Assert cleanup example: raw __assert to HSD_ASSERT
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2425 provides an example of replacing raw __assert blocks with HSD_ASSERT when the file-local line number and predicate are preserved.
source_update_candidates.reason: This supports the existing assert-report-macros standard as an example, not a new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the symbol name z for .data:0x803DBFD8 as a naming convention; the diff shows the rename in symbols.txt but the source excerpt still uses gm_803DBFD8 for StaffEntryData, and no rationale is loaded.
Do not claim fn_801AAB74 or fn_801AB200 fully matched; the automated report only shows 91.86% and 93.13% improvement respectively.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no review comments were loaded.
Do not treat PAD_STACK sizes as generally accepted style from this slice alone; the bot report is not per-tactic verifier evidence.
